name: Cloudflare GraphQL Introspection Blocking Pack
description: Detects and blocks GraphQL introspection probes targeting __schema and __type fields to reduce API schema reconnaissance exposure.
objective: Prevent unauthenticated or unapproved schema discovery attempts against GraphQL endpoints by identifying common introspection query patterns and enforcing controlled mitigation.
risk_mitigated: Attackers often use GraphQL introspection to enumerate object types, operations, and relationships, enabling faster exploit development, sensitive data discovery, and targeted abuse of API business logic.

rules.description: Match GraphQL introspection markers in URI query strings.
rules.expression: (http.request.uri.path contains "/graphql" and (lower(http.request.uri.query) contains "__schema" or lower(http.request.uri.query) contains "__type"))

rules.description: Match GraphQL introspection markers in request body payloads.
rules.expression: (http.request.uri.path contains "/graphql" and (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "__schema" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "__type"))

tuning_notes: If introspection is required for trusted users (e.g., internal tooling), scope exceptions by IP allowlist, authenticated session attributes, or dedicated non-public endpoint.
Consider limiting matching to known GraphQL paths (for example /graphql, /api/graphql) to reduce noise from unrelated traffic.
If clients submit persisted queries without raw operation names, validate observed payload shapes before broadening expressions.
Use challenge action for ambiguous traffic patterns before moving to full block in high-variance environments.
deployment_notes: Roll out in phases: start in log mode (or simulate via disabled block rule and Security Events review), then challenge mode, then enforce block after validation.
Baseline at least 7 days of normal API traffic to identify legitimate tooling or CI workflows that still rely on introspection.
Coordinate with API owners before enforcement to confirm production clients do not require runtime introspection.
Promote to block only after false positives are reviewed and explicit allow rules are in place for approved sources.
monitoring_guidance: Track Security Events for matched expressions containing __schema or __type and alert on spikes by source IP, ASN, and user agent.
Monitor block/challenge ratios and review top GraphQL endpoints hit to identify targeted reconnaissance campaigns.
Correlate detections with authentication logs and API error telemetry to confirm malicious probing versus misconfigured clients.
After enablement, periodically sample blocked payloads to validate continued effectiveness and refine exceptions safely.
